decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cards like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration for card advantage, along with efficient threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Monastery Swiftspear, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent. With powerful burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck can finish off opponents quickly. Additionally, cards like Violent Urge and Founding the Third Path provide additional ways to push through damage and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this Amulet Titan deck is to quickly ramp up mana using Amulet of Vigor and bounce lands like Simic Growth Chamber to cheat out powerful creatures like Primeval Titan. The deck focuses on utilizing lands like Urza's Saga and Crumbling Vestige alongside cards like Scapeshift and Gruul Turf to generate an immense amount of mana and enable game-winning combos. With cards like Summoner's Pact and Green Sun's Zenith to fetch key creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with large threats and generate advantage through land-based synergies.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Allosaurus Rider and Neoform to quickly assemble a powerful combo, often leading to a game-ending play. It includes cards that support the combo strategy such as Eldritch Evolution and Pact of Negation, while also incorporating cards like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Xenagos, God of Revels for additional synergy and versatility. The deck aims to quickly put pressure on the opponent and disrupt their game plan, ultimately winning through a well-timed combo or overwhelming board presence.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to deal fast damage to the opponent. The deck also includes support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Thraben Charm to control the board and protect your creatures. In addition, the deck includes cards like Ocelot Pride and Goblin Bombardment for additional damage output and removal options. The key strategy revolves around quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful spells to secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a combination of creatures and direct damage spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The key strategy involves utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, Guide of Souls, and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to establish a strong board presence and deal consistent damage. Additionally, cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional reach and removal options. The inclusion of Blood Moon can disrupt opponent's mana base, giving the deck an edge in certain matchups. Overall, the deck aims to close out games swiftly through a combination of creature beats and burn spells.

The deck focuses on aggressive and fast-paced gameplay by utilizing low-cost creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also includes cards that provide additional benefits when attacking, such as Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride. The deck includes a mix of creatures, spells, and land cards to create a balanced and versatile strategy that can adapt to different matchups. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Goblin Bombardment for extra damage and Ranger-Captain of Eos for additional creature support. The overall goal is to maintain pressure on the opponent and win through aggressive tactics.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and artifacts creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent, pushing for aggressive plays and quick victories. The deck includes a mix of combo pieces and disruptive cards to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By efficiently using artifacts and synergizing with cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and close out games before they have a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing various rituals and mana ramp cards to quickly cast spells and generate storm count. By casting multiple spells in a single turn, the deck aims to build up enough storm count to finish off the opponent with a lethal Grapeshot. The inclusion of Ral, Monsoon Mage allows for additional card draw and damage to be dealt to the opponent. The deck also includes ways to manipulate the top of the library with cards like Valakut Awakening and Wish, ensuring consistency in drawing into key combo pieces.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful combo pieces and control spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up a win condition with Goblin Charbelcher or Tameshi, Reality Architect. The absence of lands allows for a faster and more streamlined approach to the combo, with cards like Lotus Bloom and Whir of Invention serving as key components. By using a mix of countermagic, disruptive spells, and card advantage engines, the deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in the Modern format.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the Allosaurus Combo using cards like Allosaurus Rider, Eldritch Evolution, Neoform, and Summoner's Pact. The deck focuses on ramping up mana and playing big creatures like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant, and Xenagos, God of Revels to overwhelm the opponent. It also includes key support cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer for protection and disruption. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Bridgeworks Battle and Consign to Memory for additional utility and control options.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with blinking effects to generate repeatable value and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Arena of Glory, you can repeatedly blink powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to trigger their abilities multiple times. This allows you to apply pressure on your opponent while also disrupting their game plan. Consign to Memory and Prismatic Ending provide additional removal options to control the board, while cards like Quantum Riddler and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury offer additional threats to overwhelm your opponent.

The main strategy of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using a mix of counterspells, discard spells, and removal. By controlling the board and preventing the opponent from resolving threats, the deck aims to gain card advantage and eventually win the game with powerful threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Subtlety. The deck focuses on maintaining control of the game through efficient removal spells and counterspells, while also generating card advantage through card draw effects and targeted discard.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Cascade mechanic with cards like Shardless Agent and Ardent Plea to efficiently cast Living End and bring back powerful creatures from the graveyard, such as Overlord of the Balemurk and Halo Forager. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Negation and Sink into Stupor to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, there are cards like Endurance and Subtlety that provide graveyard hate and protection against removal spells. Overall, the deck aims to set up a powerful combo turn to overwhelm the opponent and win the game.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also includes burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to remove threats and push through damage. The deck runs cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls to boost the team's power and toughness, while also using powerful enchantments like The Legend of Roku and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to control the board. Overall, the deck aims to establish a fast and aggressive board presence while maintaining control of the battlefield.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo without relying on traditional lands. By using cards like Lotus Bloom and Whir of Invention to ramp up mana quickly, along with disruption cards like Disrupting Shoal and Spell Snare to control the game, the deck aims to utilize cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Thundertrap Trainer to deal massive damage to the opponent quickly. Tameshi, Reality Architect serves as a key piece in generating additional value and creating alternate win conditions. The deck is designed to be fast, aggressive, and unpredictable, catching opponents off guard with its unique landless strategy.

The main theme of this deck is fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing a combination of creatures, burn spells, and pump effects to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The deck focuses on using cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer to apply early pressure and then finish off the opponent with powerful creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury. Additionally, cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory provide additional value and support for the aggressive strategy. The deck also includes some utility cards like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm for additional reach and flexibility.
